text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill for both work and study. Good time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller steps and setting realistic daily goals. Prioritization helps you focus on the most important tasks first, so you can make steady progress. Scope management is equally important – if a project suddenly grows too large, you may need to adjust your plan or ask to reduce the workload. Finally, communication is key: if you are falling behind, inform your team or manager as early as possible. They can offer support or extend the deadline. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and meet your deadlines with confidence.
